steps.title: Recognize that both equations represent the same line
steps.content:
Multiply the first equation by 4:
$$4(7x + 6y) = 4(5)$$
$$28x + 24y = 20$$
This is exactly the second equation, so both describe the same line. Every point on $7x + 6y = 5$ satisfies both equations, giving infinitely many solutions.

steps.title: Parametrize the line by solving for x in terms of y
steps.content: Define $L(x,y)=7x+6y$. A correct parametrization must make $L(x,y)=5$ for every value of $r$. Set $r=[0,1]$ and evaluate each choice. The row for choice D returns $[5,5]$, so it stays on the solution line for both tested values.

steps.title: Confirm choice D
steps.content: For choice D, $x=-\frac{6r}{7}+\frac{5}{7}$ and $y=r$. Substituting gives $7\left(-\frac{6r}{7}+\frac{5}{7}\right)+6r=-6r+5+6r=5$, true for every $r$. Choice D is correct.
